target.title: [codex] support session-isolated app-server clients
target.body:
## Summary
- add opt-in `appServer.clientIsolation=session` so Codex app-server clients and thread bindings can be isolated per OpenClaw runtime-policy session/topic instead of shared per agent
- route run attempts, queued follow-ups, native compaction, `/btw`, `/compact`, Codex plugin commands, bound conversation turns, diagnostics, gateway compaction, and reset/delete cleanup through the same runtime-policy isolation key
- expose `appServer.turnTerminalIdleTimeoutMs` and `appServer.nativeCompaction`; keep existing defaults (`clientIsolation=agent`, native compaction on, 30 minute terminal idle timeout)

## Why
Codex app-server workers can silently stop producing terminal events (see openai/codex#21937). OpenClaw previously reused one shared Codex app-server client per agent, so a wedged worker could poison unrelated topics. This keeps old behavior by default and lets operators opt into per-session/topic isolation.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Multiple concurrent Telegram topics using Codex could eventually stall because OpenClaw reused one shared Codex app-server client per agent. When one Codex worker stopped producing terminal events, unrelated topics could inherit the same wedged client. This patch adds opt-in per-runtime-session/topic isolation with `appServer.clientIsolation="session"`.
- Real environment tested: Human-run local production OpenClaw setup on a Linux host using a user-level `openclaw-gateway` systemd service, the bundled Codex plugin, `@openai/codex` 0.130.0, and a Telegram group with multiple active topics. Hostname, account identifiers, topic ids, and tokens are redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Applied the source-equivalent patch to the installed Codex plugin bundle and manifest, enabled `appServer.clientIsolation="session"`, restarted `openclaw-gateway`, checked `systemctl --user show openclaw-gateway`, checked `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway`, then exercised multiple Telegram topics with Codex enabled.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output and runtime log excerpt from the real gateway after the patched service restart:
  ```text
  $ systemctl --user show openclaw-gateway --property=ActiveState --property=SubState --property=MainPID
  ActiveState=active
  SubState=running
  MainPID=<redacted>

  $ journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway
  [gateway] http server listening (5 plugins: acpx, codex, memory-core, openai, telegram; 5.9s)
  [gateway] ready
  ```
- Observed result after fix: Concurrent Telegram topics no longer cascade-stalled through one shared frozen Codex app-server client after enabling session isolation.
- What was not tested: Real WebSocket remote app-server isolation and non-Codex providers/extensions.

Not tested in CI/local suite:
- CI full suite clean on this machine; local `pnpm test` still fails on unrelated existing non-Codex failures listed below.

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts --reporter=dot` (1 file / 89 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts --reporter=dot` (1 file / 15 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts --reporter=dot` (2 files / 15 tests passed)
- `pnpm test src/plugins/commands.test.ts` (1 file / 42 tests passed)
- `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts` (1 file / 89 tests passed)
- `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` (4 files / 134 tests passed)
- `pnpm test src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts` (2 files / 15 tests passed)
- `pnpm test:extension codex` (50 files / 681 tests passed)
- `pnpm check` (passed)
- `pnpm build` (passed)
- `codex review --base upstream/main` (no actionable correctness issues)
- `pnpm test` (failed after 76 shards on unrelated existing non-Codex failures: Telegram durable-final proof, NVIDIA/LM Studio provider expectations, oc-path perf threshold, acpx source-vs-dist path, gateway credential/auth env expectations, Discord patch summary truncation, IPv6 loopback `EADDRNOTAVAIL ::1`, and agent/config test mocks missing unrelated exports.)

## AI-assisted
Yes. Codex implemented and locally reviewed the change; the production behavior proof above was run by a human operator on their own OpenClaw instance. I understand the code paths changed.

target.title: fix(codex): complete dynamic tool diagnostics
target.body:
Fixes #83474.

## Summary

This fixes a Codex app-server dynamic tool bookkeeping gap where `item/tool/call` requests can return a dynamic tool response, but OpenClaw does not emit a terminal tool diagnostic or clear the tracked dynamic tool call id at that request boundary.

The observed production symptom was a successful no-output `bash` dynamic tool result shown in the UI while the gateway kept reporting:

```text
reason=blocked_tool_call classification=blocked_tool_call activeTool=bash activeToolCallId=... recovery=none
```

The change makes the dynamic tool request path:

- emit `tool.execution.started` when the dynamic tool request begins;
- emit terminal `tool.execution.completed` / `tool.execution.error` when the dynamic tool response returns or throws;
- delete the tracked dynamic tool call id in `finally`;
- cover the diagnostic behavior in `run-attempt.test.ts`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83474
- Related #42112, #8288
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server dynamic `bash` tool completion was visible to the user, but gateway diagnostics kept the session in `blocked_tool_call` with `activeTool=bash` and a redacted active tool call id.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu/Linux desktop, installed OpenClaw / `@openclaw/codex` 2026.5.12 for the pre-fix failure; local patched checkout `OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (78d4183)` for after-fix diagnostic activity and patched-gateway transport attempts on May 18, 2026.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --check --experimental-strip-types extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:extensions
pnpm dlx node@24 ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
node --import tsx <redacted-diagnostic-activity-proof-script>
OPENCLAW_PROOF_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node --import tsx scripts/repro/codex-dynamic-tool-diagnostics-live-proof.mjs
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

The patched boundary now emits the terminal event that clears gateway activity state. I validated the redacted activity transition directly against the same `diagnostic-run-activity` tracker used by gateway diagnostics:

```json
{
  "afterStart": {
    "activeWorkKind": "tool_call",
    "activeToolName": "bash",
    "activeToolCallId": "tool-call-redacted",
    "activeToolAgeMs": 0,
    "lastProgressAgeMs": 0,
    "lastProgressReason": "tool:bash:started"
  },
  "afterCompleted": {
    "lastProgressAgeMs": 0,
    "lastProgressReason": "tool:bash:ended"
  }
}
```

After the terminal diagnostic, the gateway activity snapshot no longer contains `activeWorkKind=tool_call`, `activeToolName=bash`, or `activeToolCallId`.

Patched local gateway transport attempt, redacted:

```text
OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (78d4183)
[proof] setup
[proof] pair-device
[proof] start-gateway
[gateway] agent model: codex/gpt-5.4 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
[gateway] http server listening (1 plugin: codex)
[gateway] ready
[proof] gateway-call-agent
Gateway call failed: GatewayTransportError: gateway timeout after 120000ms
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:<redacted-port>
Config: <redacted-temp-config-path>
```

- Observed result after fix: Source-level patched request-boundary coverage and diagnostic activity proof show that terminal `tool.execution.completed`/`tool.execution.error` clears the active `bash` tool state that produced the stalled diagnostics. The local patched gateway starts from commit `78d4183`, but the live Codex transport did not reach tool execution in the patched runtime attempt.
- What was not tested: A successful patched live Codex `bash` tool request completing end-to-end through `gateway call agent`. The patched live attempts either disconnected before tool execution or timed out before a terminal tool event, so this PR still relies on the focused request-boundary test plus the gateway activity transition proof unless a maintainer can reproduce the live transport path.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```text
2026-05-18T07:52:04.249+02:00 [diagnostic] stalled session: sessionId=<redacted> sessionKey=<redacted> state=processing age=143s queueDepth=0 reason=blocked_tool_call classification=blocked_tool_call activeWorkKind=tool_call lastProgress=codex_app_server:notification:thread/tokenUsage/updated lastProgressAge=141s activeTool=bash activeToolCallId=<redacted> activeToolAge=148s recovery=none
2026-05-18T07:57:04.291+02:00 [diagnostic] stalled session: sessionId=<redacted> sessionKey=<redacted> state=processing age=443s queueDepth=0 reason=blocked_tool_call classification=blocked_tool_call activeWorkKind=tool_call lastProgress=codex_app_server:notification:thread/tokenUsage/updated lastProgressAge=441s activeTool=bash activeToolCallId=<redacted> activeToolAge=448s recovery=none
2026-05-18T07:57:27.172+02:00 [gateway] draining 2 active task(s) and 1 active embedded run(s) before restart with timeout 300000ms
2026-05-18T07:57:57.185+02:00 [gateway] still draining 2 active task(s) and 1 active embedded run(s) before restart
openclaw-gateway.service: State 'stop-sigterm' timed out. Killing.
```

No secrets, auth tokens, profile hashes, local usernames, session ids, full local paths, ports, or temp config paths are included in the proof above.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The `item/tool/call` branch tracked dynamic tool request state, but did not emit a terminal diagnostic event or clear the tracked dynamic tool call id at that request boundary.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered dynamic tool lookup and error behavior, but not the gateway-facing diagnostic start/end contract for this response-boundary path.
- Contributing context (if known): Related diagnostic stalls existed in the same broad area, but this report appears to be a distinct response-boundary variant.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Dynamic `item/tool/call` handling emits start and terminal diagnostic events and clears tracked dynamic tool ids.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is at a narrow app-server request boundary; the focused test exercises that boundary without depending on live Codex transport reliability.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Added in this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-backed OpenClaw sessions should stop reporting a completed dynamic tool as still active after the app-server returns the dynamic tool response.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
[item/tool/call] -> dynamic tool response -> no terminal diagnostic -> activeTool remains set

After:
[item/tool/call] -> tool.execution.started -> dynamic tool response -> terminal diagnostic -> activeTool cleared
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu/Linux desktop
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.0 locally; focused Vitest run under Node 24.15.0 because host Node 22 cannot load the repo Vitest config
- Model/provider: `openai-codex` / `codex/gpt-5.4` and `codex/gpt-5.5` attempts
- Integration/channel (if any): OpenClaw Gateway with bundled Codex app-server plugin
- Relevant config (redacted): local token auth, loopback gateway, Codex app-server `mode: yolo`, temp state/config paths redacted

### Steps

1. Reproduce the stalled active-tool diagnostics on installed OpenClaw 2026.5.12.
2. Apply the patch and run focused static/type/test validation.
3. Validate the gateway diagnostic activity transition with terminal `tool.execution.completed`.
4. Attempt a patched local gateway live Codex `bash` run; record timeout/disconnect outcome without exposing secrets.

### Expected

- Terminal dynamic tool diagnostics clear `activeTool=bash` and its tool call id.

### Actual

- Diagnostic activity proof clears the active `bash` tool state after the terminal event.
- Patched live Codex transport did not reach tool execution in this environment.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Pre-fix stalled active `bash` diagnostics; patched diagnostic activity transition; focused run-attempt regression test.
- Edge cases checked: Terminal completed/error event handling and tracked dynamic id cleanup in the patched request-boundary code path.
- What I did **not** verify: Successful patched live Codex `bash` tool completion through `gateway call agent`, because live transport timed out or disconnected before tool execution.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Prematurely clearing active tool diagnostics could hide a still-active tool call.
  - Mitigation: The patch emits terminal cleanup only after the dynamic tool response returns or throws, and the focused test covers the diagnostic event sequence.
- Risk: Live after-fix transport proof is incomplete.
  - Mitigation: The proof calls out the gap explicitly and keeps the before logs, focused regression, and activity-transition evidence redacted and reproducible.

## Validation

Passed:

```bash
node --check --experimental-strip-types extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:extensions
pnpm dlx node@24 ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
```

Focused Vitest result under Node 24:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  164 passed (164)
Duration  43.22s
```

Note: the host's system Node 22.22.0 cannot load the repo Vitest config because `path.matchesGlob` throws `Missing internal module 'internal/deps/brace-expansion'`. Running the same focused test with temporary Node 24.15.0 avoids that local runtime issue.

## AI Assistance

This PR was AI-assisted. I reviewed the issue evidence, searched for related upstream reports before filing, made the code changes locally, redacted the proof, and ran the validation commands listed above.

target.title: fix(tui): strip tagged thinking from string content
target.body:
Fixes #40736

## Summary

- strip inline `<think>...</think>` reasoning tags from TUI string content when thinking display is off
- surface tagged reasoning separately in TUI when thinking display is on
- add regression coverage for tagged-string assistant payloads in `tui-formatters`

## Root cause

TUI already handled structured reasoning blocks when assistant messages arrived as `content: [{ type: "thinking" }, { type: "text" }]`.

But some providers send reasoning inline inside plain string content using `<think>...</think>` tags. In that path, `extractContentFromMessage()` treated the whole string as visible text, so the raw reasoning leaked into the TUI even when `showThinking` was disabled.

## Testing

- `PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/node@23/bin:$PATH pnpm --dir /Users/zhangxuanyang/Desktop/DDDDAO/openclaw/worktrees/fix-tui-thinking-tag-leak exec vitest run src/tui/tui-formatters.test.ts src/tui/tui-stream-assembler.test.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts`

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: open, download, and copy
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably
- Binds paired Control UI read-token media fetches to the requested session key before serving managed image bytes
- Renders generated image frames with the same rounded clipping as other chat media
- Pre-scales known generated-image dimensions so the frame matches the visible thumbnail/image size
- Reserves pending preview space while managed image blobs load, preventing chat scroll/window jumps
- Serves timeline images from configurable thumbnail routes while keeping open/download/copy on the full image
- Adds `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` for local/managed Control UI thumbnails
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract

## Why

Generated images were being displayed without the full media affordances we expect in chat. The implementation also needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry so the new actions attach to the visible image, rounded corners clip the actual image, and the chat viewport does not jump while a managed preview is still loading.

The follow-up fixes make the preview/full-image split explicit: right-click View Image uses the thumbnail preview, while click/Open/Download/Copy use the original full-size image.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm config:schema:check`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 900s pnpm test test/vitest/vitest.acp.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.shared-core.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.tasks.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.utils.config.ts`
- Browser proof: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI chat image component in system Chromium via Playwright, then clicked Open, Download, and Copy.

## Branch Notes

This branch is based directly on latest `upstream/main` and intentionally does not include PR #75727 gateway changes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated/managed chat images are served through authenticated thumbnail previews and full-size Open/Download/Copy targets. Current head tested: `9d8852a46ca90538e1b1e2974c019530631edc88`.
- Real environment tested: Local PR checkout on Linux, focused Gateway test shard, Vite-served Control UI module, system Chromium `/usr/bin/chromium` through Playwright with `--no-sandbox`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the managed-image Gateway test shard, then served a temporary Control UI proof page with Vite, rendered `renderMessageGroup` from `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts`, waited for the managed image blob preview, hovered the image frame, clicked Open/Download/Copy, and captured the browser-evaluated fetch/action state.

target.title: perf(agents): cache provider tool schema normalization
target.body:
## Summary

- Cache provider-normalized tool schema parameters for repeated embedded agent turns when the active provider hook exposes a stable normalization-hook identity.
- Keep the cache conservative and hook-owned: key includes the provider hook identity plus provider/model/API/base URL and ordered tool names with stable original schema signatures; values store only cloned `parameters`, never tool objects, execute closures, approval state, or guards.
- Add `OPENCLAW_TOOL_SCHEMA_CACHE=0` as a kill switch, with cache-key, clone-safety, env-disable, and eviction coverage.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** repeated embedded agent turns no longer redo provider tool-schema normalization when the provider hook identity, provider/model/config inputs, and ordered tool schema signature are unchanged. Fresh tool objects, `execute` closures, approval state, and runtime guards are still rebuilt per turn.
- **Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/guanbear/workspace/openclaw-5.4-src`, branch `tool-schema-cache-pr3`, head `a5874d8ac60cf2974b74cc66b50028db103b390e`, macOS host, Node 25.9.0 via repo `pnpm` tooling.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ran `pnpm exec tsx --input-type=module` locally with a script that imports `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-schema-runtime.ts`, uses the real bundled OpenAI provider hook path, recreates 50 representative OpenAI-style tool objects and fresh `execute` closures per iteration, then runs 100 normalization iterations with `OPENCLAW_TOOL_SCHEMA_CACHE=0` and 100 iterations with the default cache enabled. Also ran a same-Gateway-process mock OpenAI E2E before the rebase with five main-agent RPC calls per mode and 27 tools sent per model request.
- **Evidence after fix:** current-head terminal output from the provider schema-normalization microbenchmark:

```json

  "head": "a5874d8ac60cf2974b74cc66b50028db103b390e",
  "cases": [
    {
      "label": "cache-disabled",

      "ms": 63625,

        "size": 0
      }
    },
    {

        "size": 1
      }
    }

}
```

Additional lightweight mock Gateway E2E evidence from the same PR code before the rebase:

- cache enabled durations `[2105, 496, 471, 465, 470]`, p50 `471ms`, 5 model requests, 27 tools per request
- cache disabled durations `[2002, 482, 472, 474, 470]`, p50 `474ms`, 5 model requests, 27 tools per request

- **Observed result after fix:** the provider schema-normalization cache is active on the real bundled OpenAI provider hook path: the benchmark stores once, then records 99 cache hits while recreating tool objects and closures every iteration. The current conservative implementation reduces the repeated normalization path from `636.25ms` to `269.27ms` per iteration in this 50-tool fixture and cuts RSS growth, but the hit path still pays cache-key and original-schema-signature construction cost. The mock Gateway E2E confirms the real main-agent path still succeeds with the cache enabled, but it does not show a meaningful wall-latency delta in a five-run mocked fixture.
- **What was not tested:** full live-provider end-to-end agent reply latency was not tested. This PR caches only provider-normalized schema `parameters`; it does not cache full tool inventory/materialization, prompt-report inputs, `core-plugin-tools`, policy resolution, or complete agent-turn startup. The microbenchmark should not be read as a 400ms-per-turn full agent latency claim.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- Current-head scoped `pnpm exec tsx` provider schema-normalization microbenchmark described above
- `git diff --check`

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: open, download, and copy
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably
- Binds paired Control UI read-token media fetches to the requested session key before serving managed image bytes
- Renders generated image frames with the same rounded clipping as other chat media
- Pre-scales known generated-image dimensions so the frame matches the visible thumbnail/image size
- Reserves pending preview space while managed image blobs load, preventing chat scroll/window jumps
- Serves timeline images from configurable thumbnail routes while keeping open/download/copy on the full image
- Adds `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` for local/managed Control UI thumbnails
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract

## Why

Generated images were being displayed without the full media affordances we expect in chat. The implementation also needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry so the new actions attach to the visible image, rounded corners clip the actual image, and the chat viewport does not jump while a managed preview is still loading.

The follow-up fixes make the preview/full-image split explicit: right-click View Image uses the thumbnail preview, while click/Open/Download/Copy use the original full-size image.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm config:schema:check`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 900s pnpm test test/vitest/vitest.acp.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.shared-core.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.tasks.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.utils.config.ts`
- Browser proof: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI chat image component in system Chromium via Playwright, then clicked Open, Download, and Copy.

## Branch Notes

This branch is based directly on latest `upstream/main` and intentionally does not include PR #75727 gateway changes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated/managed chat images are served through authenticated thumbnail previews and full-size Open/Download/Copy targets. Current head tested: `9d8852a46ca90538e1b1e2974c019530631edc88`.
- Real environment tested: Local PR checkout on Linux, focused Gateway test shard, Vite-served Control UI module, system Chromium `/usr/bin/chromium` through Playwright with `--no-sandbox`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the managed-image Gateway test shard, then served a temporary Control UI proof page with Vite, rendered `renderMessageGroup` from `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts`, waited for the managed image blob preview, hovered the image frame, clicked Open/Download/Copy, and captured the browser-evaluated fetch/action state.

commands.author: guanbear

target.title: perf(agents): cache provider tool schema normalization
target.body:
## Summary

- Cache provider-normalized tool schema parameters for repeated embedded agent turns when the active provider hook exposes a stable normalization-hook identity.
- Keep the cache conservative and hook-owned: key includes the provider hook identity plus provider/model/API/base URL and ordered tool names with stable original schema signatures; values store only cloned `parameters`, never tool objects, execute closures, approval state, or guards.
- Add `OPENCLAW_TOOL_SCHEMA_CACHE=0` as a kill switch, with cache-key, clone-safety, env-disable, and eviction coverage.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** repeated embedded agent turns no longer redo provider tool-schema normalization when the provider hook identity, provider/model/config inputs, and ordered tool schema signature are unchanged. Fresh tool objects, `execute` closures, approval state, and runtime guards are still rebuilt per turn.
- **Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/guanbear/workspace/openclaw-5.4-src`, branch `tool-schema-cache-pr3`, head `a5874d8ac60cf2974b74cc66b50028db103b390e`, macOS host, Node 25.9.0 via repo `pnpm` tooling.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ran `pnpm exec tsx --input-type=module` locally with a script that imports `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-schema-runtime.ts`, uses the real bundled OpenAI provider hook path, recreates 50 representative OpenAI-style tool objects and fresh `execute` closures per iteration, then runs 100 normalization iterations with `OPENCLAW_TOOL_SCHEMA_CACHE=0` and 100 iterations with the default cache enabled. Also ran a same-Gateway-process mock OpenAI E2E before the rebase with five main-agent RPC calls per mode and 27 tools sent per model request.
- **Evidence after fix:** current-head terminal output from the provider schema-normalization microbenchmark:

```json
{
  "head": "a5874d8ac60cf2974b74cc66b50028db103b390e",
  "cases": [
    {
      "label": "cache-disabled",
      "iterations": 100,
      "toolCount": 50,
      "ms": 63625,
      "perIterationMs": 636.25,
      "rssDeltaMb": 447.7,
      "stats": {
        "bypass": 100,
        "hit": 0,
        "miss": 0,
        "store": 0,
        "size": 0
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "cache-enabled",
      "iterations": 100,
      "toolCount": 50,
      "ms": 26927,
      "perIterationMs": 269.27,
      "rssDeltaMb": -3.1,
      "stats": {
        "bypass": 0,
        "hit": 99,
        "miss": 1,
        "store": 1,
        "size": 1
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Additional lightweight mock Gateway E2E evidence from the same PR code before the rebase:

- cache enabled durations `[2105, 496, 471, 465, 470]`, p50 `471ms`, 5 model requests, 27 tools per request
- cache disabled durations `[2002, 482, 472, 474, 470]`, p50 `474ms`, 5 model requests, 27 tools per request

- **Observed result after fix:** the provider schema-normalization cache is active on the real bundled OpenAI provider hook path: the benchmark stores once, then records 99 cache hits while recreating tool objects and closures every iteration. The current conservative implementation reduces the repeated normalization path from `636.25ms` to `269.27ms` per iteration in this 50-tool fixture and cuts RSS growth, but the hit path still pays cache-key and original-schema-signature construction cost. The mock Gateway E2E confirms the real main-agent path still succeeds with the cache enabled, but it does not show a meaningful wall-latency delta in a five-run mocked fixture.
- **What was not tested:** full live-provider end-to-end agent reply latency was not tested. This PR caches only provider-normalized schema `parameters`; it does not cache full tool inventory/materialization, prompt-report inputs, `core-plugin-tools`, policy resolution, or complete agent-turn startup. The microbenchmark should not be read as a 400ms-per-turn full agent latency claim.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- Current-head scoped `pnpm exec tsx` provider schema-normalization microbenchmark described above
- `git diff --check`

Fixes #83474.

## Summary

This fixes a Codex app-server dynamic tool bookkeeping gap where `item/tool/call` requests can return a dynamic tool response, but OpenClaw does not emit a terminal tool diagnostic or clear the tracked dynamic tool call id at that request boundary.

The observed production symptom was a successful no-output `bash` dynamic tool result shown in the UI while the gateway kept reporting:

```text
reason=blocked_tool_call classification=blocked_tool_call activeTool=bash activeToolCallId=... recovery=none
```

The change makes the dynamic tool request path:

- emit `tool.execution.started` when the dynamic tool request begins;
- emit terminal `tool.execution.completed` / `tool.execution.error` when the dynamic tool response returns or throws;
- delete the tracked dynamic tool call id in `finally`;
- cover the diagnostic behavior in `run-attempt.test.ts`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83474
- Related #42112, #8288
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server dynamic `bash` tool completion was visible to the user, but gateway diagnostics kept the session in `blocked_tool_call` with `activeTool=bash` and a redacted active tool call id.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu/Linux desktop, installed OpenClaw / `@openclaw/codex` 2026.5.12 for the pre-fix failure; local patched checkout `OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (78d4183)` for after-fix diagnostic activity and patched-gateway transport attempts on May 18, 2026.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --check --experimental-strip-types extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:extensions
pnpm dlx node@24 ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
node --import tsx <redacted-diagnostic-activity-proof-script>
OPENCLAW_PROOF_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node --import tsx scripts/repro/codex-dynamic-tool-diagnostics-live-proof.mjs
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

The patched boundary now emits the terminal event that clears gateway activity state. I validated the redacted activity transition directly against the same `diagnostic-run-activity` tracker used by gateway diagnostics:

```json
{
  "afterStart": {
    "activeWorkKind": "tool_call",
    "activeToolName": "bash",
    "activeToolCallId": "tool-call-redacted",
    "activeToolAgeMs": 0,
    "lastProgressAgeMs": 0,
    "lastProgressReason": "tool:bash:started"
  },
  "afterCompleted": {
    "lastProgressAgeMs": 0,
    "lastProgressReason": "tool:bash:ended"
  }
}
```

After the terminal diagnostic, the gateway activity snapshot no longer contains `activeWorkKind=tool_call`, `activeToolName=bash`, or `activeToolCallId`.

Patched local gateway transport attempt, redacted:

```text
OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (78d4183)
[proof] setup
[proof] pair-device
[proof] start-gateway
[gateway] agent model: codex/gpt-5.4 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
[gateway] http server listening (1 plugin: codex)
[gateway] ready
[proof] gateway-call-agent
Gateway call failed: GatewayTransportError: gateway timeout after 120000ms
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:<redacted-port>
Config: <redacted-temp-config-path>
```

- Observed result after fix: Source-level patched request-boundary coverage and diagnostic activity proof show that terminal `tool.execution.completed`/`tool.execution.error` clears the active `bash` tool state that produced the stalled diagnostics. The local patched gateway starts from commit `78d4183`, but the live Codex transport did not reach tool execution in the patched runtime attempt.
- What was not tested: A successful patched live Codex `bash` tool request completing end-to-end through `gateway call agent`. The patched live attempts either disconnected before tool execution or timed out before a terminal tool event, so this PR still relies on the focused request-boundary test plus the gateway activity transition proof unless a maintainer can reproduce the live transport path.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```text
2026-05-18T07:52:04.249+02:00 [diagnostic] stalled session: sessionId=<redacted> sessionKey=<redacted> state=processing age=143s queueDepth=0 reason=blocked_tool_call classification=blocked_tool_call activeWorkKind=tool_call lastProgress=codex_app_server:notification:thread/tokenUsage/updated lastProgressAge=141s activeTool=bash activeToolCallId=<redacted> activeToolAge=148s recovery=none
2026-05-18T07:57:04.291+02:00 [diagnostic] stalled session: sessionId=<redacted> sessionKey=<redacted> state=processing age=443s queueDepth=0 reason=blocked_tool_call classification=blocked_tool_call activeWorkKind=tool_call lastProgress=codex_app_server:notification:thread/tokenUsage/updated lastProgressAge=441s activeTool=bash activeToolCallId=<redacted> activeToolAge=448s recovery=none
2026-05-18T07:57:27.172+02:00 [gateway] draining 2 active task(s) and 1 active embedded run(s) before restart with timeout 300000ms
2026-05-18T07:57:57.185+02:00 [gateway] still draining 2 active task(s) and 1 active embedded run(s) before restart
openclaw-gateway.service: State 'stop-sigterm' timed out. Killing.
```

No secrets, auth tokens, profile hashes, local usernames, session ids, full local paths, ports, or temp config paths are included in the proof above.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The `item/tool/call` branch tracked dynamic tool request state, but did not emit a terminal diagnostic event or clear the tracked dynamic tool call id at that request boundary.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered dynamic tool lookup and error behavior, but not the gateway-facing diagnostic start/end contract for this response-boundary path.
- Contributing context (if known): Related diagnostic stalls existed in the same broad area, but this report appears to be a distinct response-boundary variant.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Dynamic `item/tool/call` handling emits start and terminal diagnostic events and clears tracked dynamic tool ids.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is at a narrow app-server request boundary; the focused test exercises that boundary without depending on live Codex transport reliability.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Added in this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-backed OpenClaw sessions should stop reporting a completed dynamic tool as still active after the app-server returns the dynamic tool response.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
[item/tool/call] -> dynamic tool response -> no terminal diagnostic -> activeTool remains set

After:
[item/tool/call] -> tool.execution.started -> dynamic tool response -> terminal diagnostic -> activeTool cleared
```

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No

- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No

- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu/Linux desktop
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.0 locally; focused Vitest run under Node 24.15.0 because host Node 22 cannot load the repo Vitest config
- Model/provider: `openai-codex` / `codex/gpt-5.4` and `codex/gpt-5.5` attempts
- Integration/channel (if any): OpenClaw Gateway with bundled Codex app-server plugin
- Relevant config (redacted): local token auth, loopback gateway, Codex app-server `mode: yolo`, temp state/config paths redacted

### Steps

1. Reproduce the stalled active-tool diagnostics on installed OpenClaw 2026.5.12.
2. Apply the patch and run focused static/type/test validation.
3. Validate the gateway diagnostic activity transition with terminal `tool.execution.completed`.
4. Attempt a patched local gateway live Codex `bash` run; record timeout/disconnect outcome without exposing secrets.

### Expected

- Terminal dynamic tool diagnostics clear `activeTool=bash` and its tool call id.

### Actual

- Diagnostic activity proof clears the active `bash` tool state after the terminal event.
- Patched live Codex transport did not reach tool execution in this environment.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Trace/log snippets

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Pre-fix stalled active `bash` diagnostics; patched diagnostic activity transition; focused run-attempt regression test.
- Edge cases checked: Terminal completed/error event handling and tracked dynamic id cleanup in the patched request-boundary code path.
- What I did **not** verify: Successful patched live Codex `bash` tool completion through `gateway call agent`, because live transport timed out or disconnected before tool execution.

## Review Conversations

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Prematurely clearing active tool diagnostics could hide a still-active tool call.
  - Mitigation: The patch emits terminal cleanup only after the dynamic tool response returns or throws, and the focused test covers the diagnostic event sequence.
- Risk: Live after-fix transport proof is incomplete.
  - Mitigation: The proof calls out the gap explicitly and keeps the before logs, focused regression, and activity-transition evidence redacted and reproducible.

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** OpenClaw's `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (headless). Starting **June 15, 2026** that path moves onto Anthropic's separate programmatic credit pool ([VentureBeat coverage](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch)) — fixed monthly allowance, billed at API rates, doesn't roll over.
- **Why it matters.** Every OpenClaw Opus turn on a Claude subscription will drain that fixed credit post–June 15 instead of counting against the interactive subscription limits subscribers are paying for. Opus turns exhaust the credit fast.
- **What changed.** New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM on a 127.0.0.1 port, taps every API SSE event, re-emits the events as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload keeps claude in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Existing `claude-cli` backend untouched. `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` user overrides inherit into the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped. System CA store is not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to the spawned claude child only). Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified. Tool-use rendering / waiting-on-tool injection lands in #82285, not here.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude` running under OpenClaw in interactive (subscription) mode with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm-installed), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/anthropic/tsconfig.json`; set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` active in user config; restart the OpenClaw gateway; send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn; observe wrapper boot in stderr, SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>`, JSONL arriving at OpenClaw; send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` to verify resume.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 5 passed (5)` — `normalizeConfig` branches (inherited claude-cli match, direct Bun-path override, env passthrough, no-override skip).
  - Gateway log excerpt + cert listing + active-config snapshot on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch. Embedded sample (one of three real turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

    ```
    cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus useResume=true session=present
    cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --model opus <prompt:N chars>
    [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
    [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 — Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
    [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
    [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill
    [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, a continuous stream of `stream_event` records, then a single `result` on `end_turn`, sets `intentionalKill = true`, and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits 0.
- **What was not tested:** Linux/macOS (developed on Windows 11 only); bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only `mcp__openclaw__*` exercised live); forced Anthropic-side `429` / `overloaded_error` mid-stream (the `api_error` synthetic event path is implemented but I did not provoke a real 429).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR is the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend); #82285 (tool-use injection, builds on the `stream_event` surface this PR establishes).

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The lack of thinking visibility on subscription is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape across four branches — inherited claude-cli match (forces `command="bun"`, stashes binary on env), direct Bun-path override (keeps the override as the spawn command, no env stash), env passthrough preservation, no-override case (env passthrough omitted so wrapper falls back to PATH lookup of `"claude"`).
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig` — fully exercised synchronously without spawning bun or claude, runs in ms vs the minutes an e2e run would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** newly-installed user with no inherited `claude-cli` override could spawn `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` stashed `merged.command` indiscriminately.
  - **Mitigation:** `normalizeConfig` consults `context.config.agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli.command`; only stashes the env passthrough on a match. Regression test asserts the env var is `undefined` for the no-override branch.
- **Risk:** `tty-spoof.cjs` is a runtime support file (not a build output) and could be omitted from packaged installs.
  - **Mitigation:** declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets`; postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper.
- **Risk:** winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install via PATHEXT on Windows.
  - **Mitigation:** wrapper spawns `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` directly when the parent backend resolved a binary, never falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- **Risk:** title-gen / Haiku side-requests inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server classifies each `/v1/messages` request into `auxiliary` (no tools) / `compaction` (compact.ts prompt markers) / `tool_followup` (last msg has tool_result) / `normal`. Wrapper drops auxiliary entirely, streams compaction as `thinking_delta`, emits a result only on `normal` `end_turn`. Response-content fingerprint backup (`<analysis>` + Primary-Request-and-Intent + Pending-Tasks) catches anything that slipped past the request-level classifier.
- **Risk:** spilled-prompt overflow file leaks to concurrent runs via shared tempdir + `--add-dir`.
  - **Mitigation:** per-run subdir under `${tmpdir()}/openclaw-interactive-proxy/run-<32-hex-bytes>/` (0700), file 0600, `--add-dir` scoped to the per-run subdir only.
- **Risk:** local CA + leaf key persisted under user's OpenClaw config dir could be reused if an attacker gets read access.
  - **Mitigation:** trust scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` only (no system trust mutation); CA issues solely the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs; key files hardened to 0600 with fail-closed regeneration; maintainer security review invited.
- **Risk:** Anthropic-side non-SSE 4xx/5xx on `/v1/messages` (429, billing, overloaded) hangs the wrapper because claude doesn't self-exit on API error.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server emits a synthetic `api_error` event for non-SSE 4xx/5xx; wrapper surfaces a `result` with `is_error: true` and exits nonzero so the CLI runner treats the turn as failed and triggers failover.
- **Risk:** two resumes of the same Claude session race on the same transcript.
  - **Mitigation:** backend config sets `serialize: true` so the framework queues same-session runs; different sessions still run concurrently. mitm-server binds both proxy stages on port 0 so concurrent proxies can't collide on a preferred port either.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** Yes — per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store untouched. Local CA + leaf key cached under user's OpenClaw config dir at 0700/0600.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token; it is forwarded byte-for-byte to the re-issued upstream request.
- **New/changed network calls:** Yes — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same session semantics.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing exfiltrated or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: existing `claude-cli` backend unchanged. Opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- New optional env: `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose wrapper stderr.
- Migration needed: no.

## Prior art

[`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) runs `claude` without `-p` but bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely — works for the raw final text but loses the `stream_event` records that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on. This PR reproduces the `-p --output-format stream-json` shape record-for-record from the SSE tap so existing OpenClaw stream consumers layer on unchanged.

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: open, download, and copy
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably
- Binds paired Control UI read-token media fetches to the requested session key before serving managed image bytes
- Renders generated image frames with the same rounded clipping as other chat media
- Pre-scales known generated-image dimensions so the frame matches the visible thumbnail/image size
- Reserves pending preview space while managed image blobs load, preventing chat scroll/window jumps
- Serves timeline images from configurable thumbnail routes while keeping open/download/copy on the full image
- Adds `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` for local/managed Control UI thumbnails
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract

## Why

Generated images were being displayed without the full media affordances we expect in chat. The implementation also needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry so the new actions attach to the visible image, rounded corners clip the actual image, and the chat viewport does not jump while a managed preview is still loading.

The follow-up fixes make the preview/full-image split explicit: right-click View Image uses the thumbnail preview, while click/Open/Download/Copy use the original full-size image.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm config:schema:check`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 900s pnpm test test/vitest/vitest.acp.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.shared-core.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.tasks.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.utils.config.ts`
- Browser proof: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI chat image component in system Chromium via Playwright, then clicked Open, Download, and Copy.

## Branch Notes

This branch is based directly on latest `upstream/main` and intentionally does not include PR #75727 gateway changes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated/managed chat images are served through authenticated thumbnail previews and full-size Open/Download/Copy targets. Current head tested: `9d8852a46ca90538e1b1e2974c019530631edc88`.
- Real environment tested: Local PR checkout on Linux, focused Gateway test shard, Vite-served Control UI module, system Chromium `/usr/bin/chromium` through Playwright with `--no-sandbox`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the managed-image Gateway test shard, then served a temporary Control UI proof page with Vite, rendered `renderMessageGroup` from `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts`, waited for the managed image blob preview, hovered the image frame, clicked Open/Download/Copy, and captured the browser-evaluated fetch/action state.

target.title: fix(gateway): recover failed lanes with missing transcripts
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: A fresh per-channel session lane with `status: "failed"` could keep reusing a `sessionId` whose persisted `sessionFile` no longer exists.
- Why it matters: Channel follow-up messages can repeatedly attempt to resume an unrecoverable CLI session and effectively hang/drop instead of recovering the lane.
- What changed: Gateway agent session reuse now treats failed sessions with a missing stored transcript file as unrecoverable, rotates to a fresh session id, and clears the stale `sessionFile` through the existing rotation path.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Fresh non-failed sessions, failed sessions with an existing transcript file, explicit reset flows, and session retention/pruning policy behavior are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83488
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Failed per-channel session lanes whose stored transcript file is missing should recover by allocating a fresh session instead of resuming the dead `sessionId`.
- Real environment tested: Linux 6.8.0 host, clean Docker `node:24-bookworm` container, repository worktree mounted at `/tmp/openclaw-83488-repro`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a real OpenClaw gateway in the clean container with isolated `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; seeded `sessions.json` with a fresh `status: "failed"` lane whose relative `sessionFile` (`missing-transcript.jsonl`) did not exist; ran `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --message proof --timeout 1 --json`; inspected the persisted session entry after the gateway handled the request.
- Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the real gateway/CLI run:

```text
gateway-log-tail:
2026-05-18T10:39:10.571+00:00 [gateway] http server listening (...)
2026-05-18T10:39:11.377+00:00 [gateway] ready

before:
{"sessionId":"failed-missing-session-id","status":"failed","sessionFile":"missing-transcript.jsonl","missing":true}

after:
{"sessionId":"962c6140-6dc1-4d33-873d-5c43ae6a1152","rotated":true,"sessionFile":"/tmp/oc-state/agents/main/sessions/962c6140-6dc1-4d33-873d-5c43ae6a1152.jsonl"}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real gateway/CLI run did not reuse `failed-missing-session-id`; it persisted a fresh session id (`962c6140-6dc1-4d33-873d-5c43ae6a1152`) and pointed `sessionFile` at the new transcript path.
- What was not tested: Live Discord plugin E2E hang/recovery; this proof exercises the gateway lane recovery behavior directly through the OpenClaw gateway and CLI.
- Before evidence:

```text
FAIL src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts > gateway agent handler > repro #83488: failed session with missing transcript is resumed instead of rotated
AssertionError: expected 'failed-missing-session-id' not to be 'failed-missing-session-id'
```

Supplemental validation after this patch:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       100 passed (100)
All matched files use the correct format.
pnpm tsgo:test:src exited 0
```

I also posted the source-level reproduction details on the issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/83488#issuecomment-4476462085

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Gateway session reuse only considered freshness (`updatedAt` / lifecycle timestamps) and the presence of `entry.sessionId`; a `failed` lane whose transcript file had disappeared could still be considered fresh and reusable.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no guard that invalidated a failed session when its stored `sessionFile` pointed at a missing file.
- Contributing context (if known): Per-channel lane recovery keeps session ids stable for fresh lanes, but this edge case needs to treat the persisted lane as unrecoverable.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `status: "failed"` + missing `sessionFile` must allocate a fresh `sessionId` and clear the stale transcript path.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The gateway agent handler is where `canReuseSession` decides whether to resume or rotate the session id.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing stale-session coverage rotates old sessions, but not fresh failed sessions whose transcript file is gone.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users on channel sessions can recover automatically from this failed-lane/missing-transcript state instead of repeatedly attempting to resume an unrecoverable session.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[channel message] -> fresh failed lane + missing transcript -> resume old sessionId -> hang/drop risk

After:
[channel message] -> failed lane + missing transcript -> rotate sessionId -> fresh run
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.8.0
- Runtime/container: Docker `node:24-bookworm`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Source-level gateway handler reproduction for per-channel lanes
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Seed a gateway session entry with `sessionId`, `status: "failed"`, fresh lifecycle timestamps, and a `sessionFile` path that does not exist.
2. Invoke the gateway agent handler for the same session key.
3. Inspect the `agentCommand` call and persisted session entry.

### Expected

- The failed/missing-transcript lane is treated as unrecoverable.
- A new session id is allocated.
- The stale `sessionFile` is cleared.

### Actual

- Before this patch, the handler reused `failed-missing-session-id`.
- After this patch, the handler rotates the session id and clears `sessionFile`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Source-level reproduction fails on current behavior.
  - Focused regression passes after the fix.
  - Full gateway agent handler test file passes (`99 passed`).
  - Touched files pass formatter check.
  - Source test TypeScript check passes (`pnpm tsgo:test:src`).
  - Local `codex review --base origin/main` returned: `No prioritized, actionable findings were identified.`
- Edge cases checked:
  - Existing stale-session rotation test still passes as part of the full file.
  - The new guard only applies when `entry.status === "failed"` and the stored `sessionFile` path is non-empty and missing.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Live Discord plugin E2E hang/recovery.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A failed session whose transcript file was intentionally removed will no longer be resumed.
  - Mitigation: That is the intended recovery behavior for this bug; the guard is limited to failed sessions with a missing persisted transcript path.

AI-assisted: yes. I used OpenClaw/Codex assistance and manually ran the reproduction and verification commands above.

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: open, download, and copy
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably
- Binds paired Control UI read-token media fetches to the requested session key before serving managed image bytes
- Renders generated image frames with the same rounded clipping as other chat media
- Pre-scales known generated-image dimensions so the frame matches the visible thumbnail/image size
- Reserves pending preview space while managed image blobs load, preventing chat scroll/window jumps
- Serves timeline images from configurable thumbnail routes while keeping open/download/copy on the full image
- Adds `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` for local/managed Control UI thumbnails
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract

## Why

Generated images were being displayed without the full media affordances we expect in chat. The implementation also needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry so the new actions attach to the visible image, rounded corners clip the actual image, and the chat viewport does not jump while a managed preview is still loading.

The follow-up fixes make the preview/full-image split explicit: right-click View Image uses the thumbnail preview, while click/Open/Download/Copy use the original full-size image.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm config:schema:check`

- Browser proof: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI chat image component in system Chromium via Playwright, then clicked Open, Download, and Copy.

## Branch Notes

This branch is based directly on latest `upstream/main` and intentionally does not include PR #75727 gateway changes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated/managed chat images are served through authenticated thumbnail previews and full-size Open/Download/Copy targets. Current head tested: `9d8852a46ca90538e1b1e2974c019530631edc88`.
- Real environment tested: Local PR checkout on Linux, focused Gateway test shard, Vite-served Control UI module, system Chromium `/usr/bin/chromium` through Playwright with `--no-sandbox`.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native subagent runs.
- Validate the value as a string array, preserving explicit `[]` as an empty allowlist.
- Forward the allowlist through `SpawnSubagentParams` into the child `agent` Gateway request so the existing embedded-runner allowlist and tool construction path apply.
- Reject `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` because ACP does not use the embedded-runner tool construction path.
- Reject `toolsAllow` for CLI-backed native runs because CLI harnesses cannot enforce OpenClaw embedded-runner tool allowlists.
- Document the user-facing option and add an Unreleased changelog entry.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can now pass a narrow native subagent tool allowlist with `toolsAllow`; the value is exposed in the live tool schema and forwarded through the child Gateway `agent` path into the existing native embedded-runner allowlist filtering.
- Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, local OpenClaw source checkout from branch `sessions-spawn-tools-allow-pr2`.
- PR head tested: `09471b37572d88702c57190bc9c0cdfccc303385`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebuilt dist with `pnpm build`, then used an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, loopback token-auth Gateway, temp workspace, temp mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server, and no live remote model/provider call. The same child Gateway `agent` path that `sessions_spawn` forwards into was run three times: without `toolsAllow`, with `toolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, and with `toolsAllow: []`.
- Evidence after fix: the mock OpenAI server captured the actual `tools` array sent by the embedded runner. Baseline sent 21 tools; the allowlisted run sent only `exec` and `read`; the explicit empty allowlist run sent 0 tools. Current-head focused tests also prove CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch for both `["read"]` and `[]`, and explicit empty embedded `toolsAllow` uses the minimal prompt path without the skills catalog.

```json
{
  "head": "09471b37572d88702c57190bc9c0cdfccc303385",
  "gatewayPort": 41167,
  "mockOpenAiBaseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:41168/v1",
  "baseline": {
    "label": "baseline",
    "runId": "pr2-proof-baseline-6262660c-a744-4330-972c-56051148dc92",
    "status": "ok",
    "wallMs": 2296,
    "toolCount": 21,
    "toolNames": [
      "edit",
      "exec",
      "image",
      "image_generate",
      "memory_get",
      "memory_search",
      "music_generate",
      "process",
      "read",
      "session_status",
      "sessions_history",
      "sessions_list",
      "sessions_send",
      "sessions_spawn",
      "sessions_yield",
      "subagents",
      "update_plan",
      "video_generate",
      "web_fetch",
      "web_search",
      "write"
    ]
  },
  "allowReadExec": {
    "label": "allow-read-exec",
    "runId": "pr2-proof-allow-read-exec-42d95c53-529a-460e-ba17-bab5ff57143b",
    "status": "ok",
    "wallMs": 440,
    "toolCount": 2,
    "toolNames": [
      "exec",
      "read"
    ]
  },
  "emptyAllow": {
    "label": "empty-allow",
    "runId": "pr2-proof-empty-allow-769c291b-1f33-4da9-afb3-644c0a1f3474",
    "status": "ok",
    "wallMs": 356,
    "toolCount": 0,
    "toolNames": []
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: final-head embedded native subagent runs enforce `toolsAllow` before the request reaches the model transport, and explicit `[]` disables tools instead of falling back to unrestricted defaults. CLI-backed native runs fail closed instead of silently ignoring the allowlist.
- Before evidence: without this PR, `sessions_spawn` had no `toolsAllow` schema entry and the spawned child Gateway `agent` request could not carry a per-spawn allowlist.
- What was not tested: a live remote model-backed spawned child run. The proof intentionally uses a mock OpenAI server to avoid real provider calls while still exercising the Gateway and embedded-runner path.

## Supplemental local timing note
The proof run also recorded local mock wall time: baseline `2296ms`, allowlisted `440ms`, empty allowlist `356ms`. This is included only as a local mock timing signal from one isolated run, not as a formal benchmark or guaranteed provider/model speedup. The primary value of this PR is correctness/control-plane narrowing; any latency win depends on tool set, runtime config, and provider behavior.

## Root Cause (if applicable)
N/A. This is additive feature plumbing over an existing embedded-runner allowlist mechanism.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects ACP runtime usage, forwards through Gateway `agent`, and reaches embedded runs started by the command runner.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: this PR connects an existing embedded-runner option to `sessions_spawn`; focused tests cover the schema, validation, and forwarding contract without requiring live model behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow the spawned native subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when the field is omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. `runtime="acp"` rejects the option with a clear input error.

## Diagram (if applicable)
N/A.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native subagent runs. ACP rejects the option because it does not use this embedded tool construction path.

## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS 26.2
- Runtime/container: Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, local source checkout
- Model/provider: mock OpenAI `/v1/responses`; no live remote model call
- Integration/channel: local loopback Gateway `agent` RPC and `agent.wait`
- Relevant config: temp workspace, temp state dir, token-auth loopback Gateway, mock provider `mock-openai/gpt-pr2-tools`

### Commands and checks
- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` passed (`5 files`, `186 tests`, 2 Vitest shards).
- `pnpm tsgo:core` passed.
- `pnpm lint:core` passed (`0 warnings and 0 errors`).
- `git diff --check` passed before the docs/changelog commit.
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed after the CLI fail-closed review-fix commit.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` printed `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`; the process then hung after printing and was killed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the current-head Gateway/mock proof.
- Final-head Gateway/mock proof above passed on `09471b37572d88702c57190bc9c0cdfccc303385`.

### Expected
- Omitted `toolsAllow`: default native child tool set is unchanged.
- `toolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`: the child model request only sees `read` and `exec` tools.
- `toolsAllow: []`: the child model request sees no tools.

### Actual
- Omitted `toolsAllow`: 21 tools captured by mock OpenAI.
- `toolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`: 2 tools captured by mock OpenAI, exactly `exec` and `read`.
- `toolsAllow: []`: 0 tools captured by mock OpenAI.

## Evidence
- [x] Gateway-backed terminal capture
- [x] Mock transport request capture
- [x] Focused tests, typecheck, lint, whitespace check

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, embedded command runner propagation, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist minimal prompt behavior, formatter/typecheck/lint/whitespace checks, final-head Gateway behavior proof.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run.

## Review Conversations
- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.
