commands.author: stainlu

target.title: Fix Codex runtime hook and session churn
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes two Codex-runtime stability problems reported by users:

- Fixes #76552: Codex no longer installs the default `PreToolUse` native hook relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, avoiding one `openclaw hooks relay ... --event pre_tool_use` child process per native Codex tool call.
- Fixes #76179: Codex no longer throws away the only saved app-server thread when the dynamic tool catalog changes. It keeps reusable Codex thread bindings per dynamic-tool fingerprint, so owner/non-owner tool availability flips start each catalog once and then resume the matching native thread.

## Root Cause

For #76552, current `main` already avoided relay subprocesses for `PostToolUse` and finalization hooks when no local handlers exist, but `pre_tool_use` still returned `true` unconditionally in `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts`. Default Codex app-server config could therefore register a `PreToolUse` command hook that shells out through the OpenClaw relay for every native tool use, even when there was no OpenClaw `before_tool_call` policy to run.

For #76179, the Codex app-server thread fingerprint included the dynamic tool registration set. Owner-only tools are filtered out for non-owner senders, so an owner/non-owner context change can alter the registered dynamic tool names. Before this PR, a mismatch cleared the single stored Codex binding, so alternating owner/non-owner turns could continuously create fresh native Codex sessions.

## What Changed

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` now reports local `pre_tool_use` relay work only when `hasBeforeToolCallPolicy()` is true. `permission_request` remains active because Codex approvals still need the app-server bridge.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.ts` can persist alternate Codex app-server thread bindings for the same OpenClaw session.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts` selects a binding compatible with the current dynamic-tool fingerprint. If none exists, it starts a new binding without deleting other compatible bindings.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` reselects the startup binding after dynamic tools are built, so context/startup decisions use the matching per-catalog Codex thread.
- Owner-only tools stay filtered for non-owner Codex thread starts.

## Before / After

Before:

- Default Codex sessions could register a `PreToolUse` relay command even with no `before_tool_call` plugin policy, so multiple native tools could fan out into multiple OpenClaw relay CLI child processes.
- Switching between owner and non-owner senders could change dynamic tool registration and repeatedly replace the saved Codex native thread binding.

After:

- No `PreToolUse` relay command is installed unless there is actual before-tool policy work.
- Command approvals still get the in-process `nativeHookRelay` bridge for `pre_tool_use` decisions.
- Owner-only schemas are not sent to non-owner Codex thread starts.
- Owner and non-owner dynamic-tool catalogs get separate reusable Codex thread bindings: first flip starts the second catalog, later flips resume the matching existing thread.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex runtime process/session churn. Default app-server runs should not install a `PreToolUse` relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, and owner-only dynamic tool availability changes should not continuously replace Codex native sessions.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout on May 18, 2026. Private home/workspace paths and auth details are redacted from the older live-run transcript below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: On current PR head `7109e930184ca19f68217d7a217f7f3b9eecd599`, ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`, `pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/config.shared-auth.test.ts`, and `env OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=extensions pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`. Earlier on this PR branch, ran real `pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 ...` turns through the Codex app-server harness.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied terminal output is embedded below under `Current-head verification`, `Production-code runtime probe`, and `Live Codex app-server smoke`.
- Observed result after fix: the owner catalog and non-owner catalog start separate Codex threads once, then resume matching bindings on later flips; the non-owner `thread/start` payload omits owner-only tools; current-head tests and prompt snapshot check pass.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live external chat-channel ingress scenario that naturally flips `senderIsOwner`; that path needs real channel identity setup. The same owner/non-owner availability flip is covered through the production Codex thread lifecycle code path and app-server tests.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): source-level root cause is described above; no separate live before-run was captured because the original failure mode can create many relay child processes on a small machine.

No product UI screenshot is applicable here because the bugs are runtime process/session churn in the Codex app-server path. Terminal/runtime output is the relevant proof artifact.

### Current-head verification

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
7109e930184ca19f68217d7a217f7f3b9eecd599

$ git diff --stat upstream/main...HEAD
10 files changed, 420 insertions(+), 97 deletions(-)

$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
# no output

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  198 passed (198)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  112 passed (112)

$ pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/config.shared-auth.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  9 passed (9)

$ env OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=extensions pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).
```

The direct hook relay test needs a loopback listener; inside the Codex sandbox it fails with `listen EPERM: operation not permitted 127.0.0.1`, so that file was rerun outside the sandbox. The outside-sandbox run passed.

### Production-code runtime probe

This earlier probe directly exercised production `startOrResumeThread` and session-binding behavior on this PR, using owner tools `[message, cron]` and non-owner tools `[message]`:

```json
{
  "requestMethods": [
    "thread/start",
    "thread/start",
    "thread/resume",
    "thread/resume"
  ],
  "startDynamicTools": [
    [
      "message",
      "cron"
    ],
    [
      "message"
    ]
  ],
  "resumeThreadIds": [
    "thread-1",
    "thread-2"
  ],
  "storedBindings": [
    {
      "threadId": "thread-2",
      "dynamicToolsFingerprintPresent": true
    },
    {
      "threadId": "thread-1",
      "dynamicToolsFingerprintPresent": true
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result: the owner catalog and non-owner catalog start separate Codex threads once, then resume `thread-1` and `thread-2` on later flips. The non-owner `thread/start` payload only contained `message`, not `cron`.

### Live Codex app-server smoke

Earlier on this PR branch, I ran two real local Codex app-server OpenClaw turns with the same session id. That verified the actual app-server harness/auth path and same-thread resume behavior:

```text
$ codex login status
Logged in using ChatGPT

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK"

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK2" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK2"
```

## Regression Coverage

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`: no-handler relay state no longer enables `pre_tool_use`; `before_tool_call` still enables `PreToolUse`; `after_tool_call` does not accidentally enable `PreToolUse`.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts`: alternate dynamic-tool thread bindings persist beside the same OpenClaw session file.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`: non-owner Codex thread starts omit owner-only dynamic tools, and owner/non-owner flips start each tool catalog once before resuming matching thread bindings.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`: side-question/permission-request coverage keeps permission relay behavior while confirming default `PreToolUse` stays empty when no before-tool policy exists.

Broad Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because the Blacksmith/Testbox CLI/env/profile were not available in this environment. The focused affected suites above are green locally, and the latest push triggers exact-head GitHub CI.

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: open, download, and copy
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably
- Binds paired Control UI read-token media fetches to the requested session key before serving managed image bytes
- Renders generated image frames with the same rounded clipping as other chat media
- Pre-scales known generated-image dimensions so the frame matches the visible thumbnail/image size
- Reserves pending preview space while managed image blobs load, preventing chat scroll/window jumps
- Serves timeline images from configurable thumbnail routes while keeping open/download/copy on the full image
- Adds `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` for local/managed Control UI thumbnails
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract

## Why

Generated images were being displayed without the full media affordances we expect in chat. The implementation also needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry so the new actions attach to the visible image, rounded corners clip the actual image, and the chat viewport does not jump while a managed preview is still loading.

The follow-up fixes make the preview/full-image split explicit: right-click View Image uses the thumbnail preview, while click/Open/Download/Copy use the original full-size image.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm config:schema:check`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 900s pnpm test test/vitest/vitest.acp.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.shared-core.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.tasks.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.utils.config.ts`
- Browser proof: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI chat image component in system Chromium via Playwright, then clicked Open, Download, and Copy.

## Branch Notes

This branch is based directly on latest `upstream/main` and intentionally does not include PR #75727 gateway changes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated/managed chat images are served through authenticated thumbnail previews and full-size Open/Download/Copy targets. Current head tested: `9d8852a46ca90538e1b1e2974c019530631edc88`.
- Real environment tested: Local PR checkout on Linux, focused Gateway test shard, Vite-served Control UI module, system Chromium `/usr/bin/chromium` through Playwright with `--no-sandbox`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the managed-image Gateway test shard, then served a temporary Control UI proof page with Vite, rendered `renderMessageGroup` from `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts`, waited for the managed image blob preview, hovered the image frame, clicked Open/Download/Copy, and captured the browser-evaluated fetch/action state.

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** OpenClaw's `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (headless). Starting **June 15, 2026** that path moves onto Anthropic's separate programmatic credit pool ([VentureBeat coverage](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch)) — fixed monthly allowance, billed at API rates, doesn't roll over.
- **Why it matters.** Every OpenClaw Opus turn on a Claude subscription will drain that fixed credit post–June 15 instead of counting against the interactive subscription limits subscribers are paying for. Opus turns exhaust the credit fast.
- **What changed.** New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM on a 127.0.0.1 port, taps every API SSE event, re-emits the events as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload keeps claude in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Existing `claude-cli` backend untouched. `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` user overrides inherit into the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped. System CA store is not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to the spawned claude child only). Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified. Tool-use rendering / waiting-on-tool injection lands in #82285, not here.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude` running under OpenClaw in interactive (subscription) mode with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm-installed), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/anthropic/tsconfig.json`; set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` active in user config; restart the OpenClaw gateway; send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn; observe wrapper boot in stderr, SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>`, JSONL arriving at OpenClaw; send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` to verify resume.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 5 passed (5)` — `normalizeConfig` branches (inherited claude-cli match, direct Bun-path override, env passthrough, no-override skip).
  - Gateway log excerpt + cert listing + active-config snapshot on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch. Embedded sample (one of three real turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

    ```
    cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus useResume=true session=present
    cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --model opus <prompt:N chars>
    [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
    [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 — Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
    [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
    [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill
    [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, a continuous stream of `stream_event` records, then a single `result` on `end_turn`, sets `intentionalKill = true`, and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits 0.
- **What was not tested:** Linux/macOS (developed on Windows 11 only); bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only `mcp__openclaw__*` exercised live); forced Anthropic-side `429` / `overloaded_error` mid-stream (the `api_error` synthetic event path is implemented but I did not provoke a real 429).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR is the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend); #82285 (tool-use injection, builds on the `stream_event` surface this PR establishes).

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The lack of thinking visibility on subscription is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape across four branches — inherited claude-cli match (forces `command="bun"`, stashes binary on env), direct Bun-path override (keeps the override as the spawn command, no env stash), env passthrough preservation, no-override case (env passthrough omitted so wrapper falls back to PATH lookup of `"claude"`).
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig` — fully exercised synchronously without spawning bun or claude, runs in ms vs the minutes an e2e run would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** newly-installed user with no inherited `claude-cli` override could spawn `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` stashed `merged.command` indiscriminately.
  - **Mitigation:** `normalizeConfig` consults `context.config.agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli.command`; only stashes the env passthrough on a match. Regression test asserts the env var is `undefined` for the no-override branch.
- **Risk:** `tty-spoof.cjs` is a runtime support file (not a build output) and could be omitted from packaged installs.
  - **Mitigation:** declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets`; postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper.
- **Risk:** winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install via PATHEXT on Windows.
  - **Mitigation:** wrapper spawns `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` directly when the parent backend resolved a binary, never falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- **Risk:** title-gen / Haiku side-requests inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server classifies each `/v1/messages` request into `auxiliary` (no tools) / `compaction` (compact.ts prompt markers) / `tool_followup` (last msg has tool_result) / `normal`. Wrapper drops auxiliary entirely, streams compaction as `thinking_delta`, emits a result only on `normal` `end_turn`. Response-content fingerprint backup (`<analysis>` + Primary-Request-and-Intent + Pending-Tasks) catches anything that slipped past the request-level classifier.
- **Risk:** spilled-prompt overflow file leaks to concurrent runs via shared tempdir + `--add-dir`.
  - **Mitigation:** per-run subdir under `${tmpdir()}/openclaw-interactive-proxy/run-<32-hex-bytes>/` (0700), file 0600, `--add-dir` scoped to the per-run subdir only.
- **Risk:** local CA + leaf key persisted under user's OpenClaw config dir could be reused if an attacker gets read access.
  - **Mitigation:** trust scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` only (no system trust mutation); CA issues solely the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs; key files hardened to 0600 with fail-closed regeneration; maintainer security review invited.
- **Risk:** Anthropic-side non-SSE 4xx/5xx on `/v1/messages` (429, billing, overloaded) hangs the wrapper because claude doesn't self-exit on API error.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server emits a synthetic `api_error` event for non-SSE 4xx/5xx; wrapper surfaces a `result` with `is_error: true` and exits nonzero so the CLI runner treats the turn as failed and triggers failover.
- **Risk:** two resumes of the same Claude session race on the same transcript.
  - **Mitigation:** backend config sets `serialize: true` so the framework queues same-session runs; different sessions still run concurrently. mitm-server binds both proxy stages on port 0 so concurrent proxies can't collide on a preferred port either.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** Yes — per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store untouched. Local CA + leaf key cached under user's OpenClaw config dir at 0700/0600.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token; it is forwarded byte-for-byte to the re-issued upstream request.
- **New/changed network calls:** Yes — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same session semantics.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing exfiltrated or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: existing `claude-cli` backend unchanged. Opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- New optional env: `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose wrapper stderr.
- Migration needed: no.

## Prior art

[`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) runs `claude` without `-p` but bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely — works for the raw final text but loses the `stream_event` records that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on. This PR reproduces the `-p --output-format stream-json` shape record-for-record from the SSE tap so existing OpenClaw stream consumers layer on unchanged.

target.title: feat(mattermost): add /model dialog picker
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Mattermost `/oc_model` and `/oc_models` still relied on the older message picker, so they could not use Mattermost's richer dialog flow.
- Why it matters: the Discord picker already feels native and multi-step; Mattermost needed a comparable native picker path instead of staying button-first.
- What changed: plain `/oc_model` and `/oc_models` now open a Mattermost dialog when `trigger_id` is available, dialog refresh/submit callbacks stay inside the Mattermost extension, and the final apply still routes through the existing shared `/model` command path.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no shared interactive-core expansion, no unrelated config/schema work, and the older message picker still remains as fallback when a dialog cannot be opened.

## Links

- Issue/PR refs: none

## Verification

- `CI=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/interactions.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.test.ts`
- `CI=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts`
- Rebased onto current `origin/main` and resolved the single overlapping `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.ts` import conflict from current main.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Mattermost native `/oc_model` slash-command routing and dialog callback reachability on the live devbox-to-vp deployment that backs this plugin test setup.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw gateway running from this worktree on `devbox`, published through Tailscale Serve at `https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net`; Mattermost running on `vp` and calling the gateway over that public tailnet URL.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Restart the gateway from the `codex-mm-model-dialog` worktree on `devbox`.
  2. Let Mattermost native `oc_*` commands register against `https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net/api/channels/mattermost/command`.
  3. Probe the slash callback from the Mattermost host and verify it responds as a live POST-only endpoint.
  4. Run plain `/oc_model` in the Mattermost workspace tied to that deployment.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted runtime logs and copied live output from the real deployment.

```text
11:37:01 [mattermost] registered command /oc_model (id=rr9eyu8daif6p87czrtkwxb6fe)
11:37:02 [mattermost] slash commands activated for account default (8 commands)
11:37:02 [mattermost] slash commands registered (8 commands across 1 teams, callback=https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net/api/channels/mattermost/command)
```

```text
$ curl -k -sS --connect-timeout 5 --max-time 10 -D - https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net/api/channels/mattermost/command
HTTP/2 405
allow: POST
```

- Observed result after fix: The live deployment registered native Mattermost commands against the reachable devbox callback URL instead of the old `localhost` route, the callback was reachable from the Mattermost VPS, and the live tester on that deployment reported `/oc_model` working after the routing and channel-lookup fixes. This follow-up branch update also carries the same verified slash channel facts through dialog submit when later channel lookup is forbidden.
- What was not tested: A fresh screenshot or video of the post-rebase dialog submit flow is not attached here; the new submit-fallback behavior itself is locked by the added regression coverage in `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.test.ts` and `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts`, and the broader rebased Mattermost shard including `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts` and `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.test.ts` stays green.

## Behavior Notes

- The new picker is Mattermost-owned UI: dialog open, refresh, and submit live in `extensions/mattermost/**`.
- If Mattermost does not provide a usable `trigger_id`, OpenClaw falls back to the existing message-based picker instead of breaking `/oc_model`.
- Slash-command authorization now falls back to channel information already present in the slash payload when `GET /channels/<id>` is forbidden, and dialog submit now reuses the signed fallback channel snapshot for the same restricted-channel case.

target.title: feat(feishu): add agent message delete and recall
target.body:
# PR 1 draft: Feishu agent message delete and recall

Title:

```text
feat(feishu): add agent message delete and recall actions
```

## Summary

- Add an agent-callable `feishu_message` tool for `list`, `delete`, `recall`, `read_receipts`, and `read_users`.
- Wire Feishu `messages` tool config through defaults, schema validation, plugin metadata, API exports, and runtime registration.
- Expose Telegram-like generic message actions for Feishu: `delete` and `unsend`.
- Track the latest bot-sent Feishu message per account/chat so natural-language requests like "delete your previous message" can work even when the model omits `messageId`.
- Document Feishu message-management permissions, Unix-second time filters, and delete/recall behavior.

## Related upstream work

- Closes / revives https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/51422
- Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/52993
- Complements, but does not replace, inbound recall-event work in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/55225 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/55691
- Leaves Feishu reaction tool registration for a follow-up PR, because reaction fixes are already active in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/66478 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77653

## Fixes compared with #52993

- `start_time` and `end_time` are documented and validated as Unix-second timestamp strings, matching Feishu's API, instead of ISO 8601 strings.
- `delete` and `recall` have distinct request paths and user-facing messages; delete no longer reuses recall semantics such as "cannot recall after two minutes".
- Missing Feishu scopes are surfaced with actionable messages, including `im:message.group_msg` for group-message deletion.

## Notes for reviewers

- Feishu delete behaves as a platform soft-delete/recall: follow-up reads can return `deleted: true`, `updated: true`, and empty content. Some clients may still show a deletion placeholder.
- The last-bot-message fallback is intentionally in-memory and scoped by account/chat with a 24-hour TTL. It only records messages sent by this bot process.
- The feature is gated by `tools.messages`, defaulting to `false` because message list/delete/recall/read-receipts are sensitive.

## Testing

```text
corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/message-tool.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts
# 4 files, 104 tests passed

corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts
# 2 files, 84 tests passed

corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts
# 63 files, 786 tests passed

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# passed

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
# passed

corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...
# passed

corepack pnpm exec oxlint extensions/feishu/src/sent-message-cache.ts extensions/feishu/src/send-target.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.ts extensions/feishu/src/media.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts
# passed, 0 warnings/errors

git diff --check
# passed
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu agents can delete or recall bot messages from a real group chat through the new `feishu_message` tool and generic `message(action="delete"|"unsend")` path.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 host, local OpenClaw gateway from this branch, enterprise self-built Feishu app using WebSocket mode, real Feishu group chat with the bot added, and Feishu app scopes including `im:message:update`, `im:message:readonly`, and `im:message.group_msg`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Enabled the opt-in message management gate for the Feishu test account, started the local gateway from this branch with `openclaw gateway run --force`, sent messages in the real Feishu group, first verified delete failed before `im:message.group_msg` was granted, granted the scope and restarted the gateway, sent a live bot reply, asked the bot to delete its previous message, then checked the deleted message through Feishu message read output.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output and redacted runtime notes from the real Feishu setup:

```text
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
Feishu WebSocket ready at 2026-05-18T15:43:04+08:00

Initial group delete attempt:
  Feishu API error code=230027
  msg="need scope: im:message.group_msg"
  log_id=20260518150333B37818A84E685EE4FE60

After granting im:message.group_msg:
  sent message_id=om_x100b6f9fbdc2c8e4c3bb0f1440e9c1d
  im.message.delete response={"code":0,"msg":"success"}
  follow-up message read:
    deleted=true
    updated=true
    content_length=0

Natural-language group flow after last-bot-message fallback:
  user: delete your previous message
  agent called message(action="delete") without messageId
  fallback resolved last bot message in chat oc_* to om_*
  Feishu delete returned deleted=true
```

Observed result after fix: The bot replied in the Feishu group, deleted its own previous message after the required group-message scope was enabled, and Feishu readback showed the target message was deleted with empty content. The natural-language "delete your previous message" case also worked without the model supplying a `messageId`.

What was not tested: No additional gaps

commands.author: jasonyliu

target.title: /reset preserves claude-cli --resume binding, causing context to silently rebound on the next turn
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: a user `/reset` on an OpenClaw session that flows through the gateway (TUI reset, webchat reset button, or any `sessions.reset` API call) rotates OpenClaw's own session metadata but does not drop the claude-cli `--resume` binding. The next turn after reset spawns `claude` with `--resume <oldSessionId>`, Anthropic reloads the full prior transcript from the prompt cache, and the OpenClaw token meter rebounds to roughly the pre-reset level within one or two turns.
- Why it matters: `/reset` is functionally inert against the claude-cli side for non-subagent sessions. The TUI briefly shows `0/1.0m`, which looks correct, then climbs back to the pre-reset level. Across a couple of resets the symptom looks like "OpenClaw is lying about my context size" — corrosive to runtime trust.
- What changed: in `performGatewaySessionReset` (`src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts`), after building the post-reset `nextEntry`, call the existing `clearAllCliSessions(nextEntry)` helper from `src/agents/cli-session.ts` — but only when the session is not a spawned subagent (detected by `isSubagentSessionKey(primaryKey)` from `src/routing/session-key.ts`, the canonical helper already imported and used elsewhere in the file). Subagent sessions (canonical `:subagent:` keys) continue to preserve their CLI bindings, honouring Tak Hoffman's deliberate `fix(regression): preserve CLI bindings across session reset` (commit `fa56682b3ced`, March 27 2026). User-facing parent-linked sessions that are NOT subagents (e.g. dashboard children that happen to set `parentSessionKey`) are not exempt — their bindings are cleared, matching user-intuitive `/reset` semantics.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - The non-reset continuation path in `src/agents/get-reply.ts` correctly preserves these same three fields — required for `--resume` continuity across normal turns. Left untouched.
  - The subagent path in `performGatewaySessionReset` itself: bindings preserved, matching Tak's contract. The existing `sessions.reset preserves spawned session ownership metadata` test in `src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-models.test.ts` passes unchanged.
  - The auto-reply `/reset` and `/new` path in `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts` already clears these three fields unconditionally after Vincent Koc's `fix(agents): rotate claude cli bindings on reset` (commit `7cd015b20398`, April 5 2026). This PR brings the gateway path closer to that contract for the non-subagent case.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #81003 — proposes a new opt-in `sessions.hardReset` action with a `discardResumeToken` flag. Complementary; this PR fixes the existing `/reset` so the contract matches user intuition for non-subagent sessions without requiring a new action.
- Related #81821 — gates `cliSessionBinding` persist on transcript flush (write-side); this PR clears the binding on reset (read-side at next spawn). Different code paths, same family of "stale binding produces wrong behavior" issues.
- Builds on top of `fa56682b3ced` (Takhoffman) and `7cd015b20398` (vincentkoc).
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `/reset` on a non-subagent session does not actually clear the claude-cli session on the Anthropic side; the next turn re-resumes the old session and the token meter rebounds.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw `2026.4.23` (locally hot-patched dist bundle to mirror the source fix), macOS 14.8.x arm64, Claude Code runner `claude-opus-4-7`, agent `agent:main:axiomyx`, workspace under `~/.openclaw/workspace`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Restart the gateway so the patched module is live (`launchctl bootout gui/$UID/ai.openclaw.gateway && launchctl bootstrap gui/$UID ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist`).
  2. In a session previously at ~7×× k / 1.0m context, run `/reset`.
  3. Send a follow-up message.
  4. Inspect `sessions.json` for the agent and the spawned `claude-cli` session file under `~/.claude/projects/<workspace>/`.
- Evidence after fix (live observations from the local 4.23 install):
  - Post-reset entry in `sessions.json` no longer carries `claudeCliSessionId`, `cliSessionBindings`, or `cliSessionIds` for the (non-subagent) main session. After the first post-reset turn, the post-run hook in `src/agents/command/session-store.ts` writes a **new** `claudeCliSessionId` back into the entry (different from the pre-reset value).
  - A new `~/.claude/projects/<workspace>/<newSessionId>.jsonl` exists, small (one user prompt + one assistant turn).
  - The old `<oldSessionId>.jsonl` is orphaned on disk — preserved for archival, no longer loaded.
  - TUI token meter stays at bootstrap baseline (`71k / 1.0m (~7%)` on my workspace, dominated by AGENTS.md + skills + tool schemas) across many turns after reset, instead of rebounding.
- Inspectable artifacts (live, redacted) demonstrating the fix:

  *Pre-patch state still pinned on `agent:main:axiomyx` (no post-patch `/reset` has run on this entry yet; preserved as a frozen "before" snapshot — the local hotpatch only fixes future resets):*

  ```json
  // sessions.json excerpt
  {
    "sessionId": "5a2b4ee5-…",
    "claudeCliSessionId": "512bb4c4-6535-4531-8689-7e38c242cd12",
    "cliSessionBindings": { "claude-cli": { "sessionId": "512bb4c4-…", ... } },
    "cliSessionIds": { "claude-cli": "512bb4c4-…" }
  }
  ```

  ```
  // two pre-patch sessions.reset events on this entry — binding survived both
  2026-05-13T13:57:44.075+08:00 [ws] ⇄ res ✓ sessions.reset 287ms ...
  2026-05-14T17:25:07.550+08:00 [ws] ⇄ res ✓ sessions.reset 290ms ...

  // claude-cli transcript at the bound id — started 2026-05-11, still being --resume'd as of 2026-05-18 18:45
  $ stat -f "size=%z mtime=%Sm" ~/.claude/projects/<workspace>/512bb4c4-….jsonl
  size=7997299  mtime=2026-05-18 18:45
  $ head -1 ~/.claude/projects/<workspace>/512bb4c4-….jsonl
  { ..., "timestamp": "2026-05-11T09:30:41.552Z", ... }
  ```

  *Post-patch `/reset` on a different non-subagent workspace agent (`agent:main:openclaw`), local hotpatch active:*

  ```
  // reset at 14:23:12, next spawn 12s later
  2026-05-16T14:23:12.325+08:00 [ws] ⇄ res ✓ sessions.reset 1444ms ...
  2026-05-16T14:23:24.838+08:00 [agent/cli-backend] claude live session start: ...

  // brand-new claude-cli transcript at the fresh id minted post-reset
  $ stat -f "size=%z mtime=%Sm" ~/.claude/projects/<workspace>/3da3f531-….jsonl
  size=1988080  mtime=2026-05-18 19:30
  $ head -1 ~/.claude/projects/<workspace>/3da3f531-….jsonl
  { ..., "timestamp": "2026-05-16T06:23:25.852Z", ... }
  ```

  ```json
  // post-reset sessions.json — fresh claudeCliSessionId, compactionCount reset
  {
    "sessionId": "3a743367-…",
    "claudeCliSessionId": "3da3f531-c9eb-44f5-945f-1ed3dd7615ff",
    "cliSessionBindings": { "claude-cli": { "sessionId": "3da3f531-…", ... } },
    "cliSessionIds": { "claude-cli": "3da3f531-…" },
    "compactionCount": 0
  }
  ```

  Net: the May 16 post-patch `/reset` properly rotated the claude-cli session (`3da3f531-…` ≠ pre-reset id), the bound transcript file on disk was created 13 seconds after the reset, the old transcript is orphaned, and `compactionCount` reset to 0. The May 13 / May 14 pre-patch resets did neither — same binding, same growing file.
- Local CI proof from this branch:
  - `pnpm install` ✓ (25.3s)
  - `pnpm build` ✓
  - `pnpm check` ✓ (0 warnings, 0 errors across 5400 files; all policy guards pass)
  - Targeted tests on touched files (`src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts` + `src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-models.test.ts` + `src/agents/cli-session.test.ts`): **33/33 pass**, 3.32s. Three new tests cover all three branches of the guard: non-subagent main session (cleared), dashboard-child (`parentSessionKey` set, key without `:subagent:` infix — cleared), spawned subagent (preserved). Tak's pre-existing `sessions.reset preserves spawned session ownership metadata` test in `reset-models.test.ts` also passes unchanged.
  - Full `pnpm test`: 31 pre-existing failures across 5 CLI/plugin files (`src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`, `src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts`, `src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts`, `src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts`, `src/plugins/uninstall.test.ts`). **Verified pre-existing on `upstream/main`** by stashing this branch and re-running `update-cli.test.ts` — still 23 failures, unrelated to this change. Also note `check-test-types` failure on `src/cron/isolated-agent.model-overrides.test.ts` is a pre-existing TypeScript issue about OpenAI provider config typing. Per CONTRIBUTING.md guidance not to chase known-main failures in this PR.
- Observed result after fix: `/reset` now produces the expected clean-slate semantics on both sides for non-subagent sessions. No rebound.
- What was not tested: behaviour under concurrent reset + dispatch (covered by existing locks, not exercised here); ACP-backed sessions end-to-end (the change is in the synchronous `nextEntry` construction so it applies uniformly, but I didn't run an ACP scenario).
- Before evidence: on the pre-patch dist, the same `/reset` left all three fields populated in `sessions.json` and the bound claude-cli session file at `<oldSessionId>.jsonl` continued to grow across two separate reset boundaries (May 13 05:57 UTC and May 14 09:25 UTC on my agent), proving the spawned claude was being re-`--resume`d on the same id.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: in the post-reset `nextEntry` construction inside `performGatewaySessionReset` (`src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts`), three claude-cli binding fields are carried forward verbatim from `currentEntry`. The cli-runner later reads those via `getCliSessionId(entry, provider)` (`src/agents/cli-session.ts`) to decide whether to pass `--resume`. Because the bindings were preserved unconditionally, `useResume` evaluated true on the very next spawn even for non-subagent main sessions, and the prior conversation was reloaded from prompt cache on Anthropic's side.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no test asserted that `performGatewaySessionReset` drops the binding fields for non-subagent sessions. The bug is invisible to existing tests because OpenClaw's own token-meter and session-id state reset correctly — only a real claude-cli-side post-reset turn surfaces it. The new test in this PR locks in that guardrail (alongside a sibling test confirming the subagent preservation contract is unchanged).
- Contributing context: Tak Hoffman's earlier `fa56682b3ced` introduced unconditional preservation of these three fields to fix a subagent-specific regression (his only test change is the subagent ownership-metadata test). Vincent Koc's later `7cd015b20398` moved the auto-reply `/reset` and `/new` path to unconditional clearing. This PR aligns the gateway path with the auto-reply path's contract for the non-subagent case, while preserving Tak's subagent protection.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: three new tests in `src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts`, using the same `setupGatewaySessionsTestHarness()` harness as the existing reset-hook coverage.
- Scenarios the tests lock in:
  1. Starting from a non-subagent session entry with `claudeCliSessionId`, `cliSessionBindings`, and `cliSessionIds` populated for `claude-cli`, after a successful `sessions.reset` the new entry under `agent:main:main` has `sessionId` rotated and all three binding fields read `undefined` (the bug fix guardrail).
  2. Starting from a parent-linked **non-subagent** session entry (key `dashboard:child:42` → canonical `agent:main:dashboard:child:42`, no `:subagent:` infix, with `parentSessionKey` set), after `sessions.reset` the new entry has `sessionId` rotated and all three binding fields read `undefined` (the dashboard-child guardrail — locks in the tighter predicate so a future loosening doesn't reintroduce the bug for parent-linked-but-not-subagent sessions).
  3. Starting from a spawned-subagent session entry (canonical key with `:subagent:` infix, with `parentSessionKey`, `spawnedBy`, `subagentRole`, plus the same binding fields), after `sessions.reset` the new entry has `sessionId` rotated but all three binding fields are still populated with the pre-reset values (the Tak contract guardrail).
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it tests the actual reset surface (`directSessionReq("sessions.reset", ...)`), uses the existing harness so setup cost is near zero, and reads the post-reset entry back via `loadSessionStore` so a future regression in any of the three branches of the guard predicate surfaces immediately.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): the `clearAllCliSessions` helper itself is exercised in `src/agents/cli-session.test.ts` ("clears provider-scoped and global CLI session state"). That covered the helper but not its conditional invocation from the reset path, which is exactly the gap this bug fell through.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

For non-subagent sessions only: `/reset` and `/new` (when routed through the gateway path — TUI reset, webchat reset, `sessions.reset` API) now actually clear the claude-cli `--resume` binding, so the next turn after reset spawns a fresh claude session on the Anthropic side. The TUI token meter no longer rebounds to the pre-reset level — it stays at bootstrap baseline. The old claude-cli session transcript on disk becomes orphaned (preserved, not loaded).

Spawned subagent sessions (canonical session keys carrying the `:subagent:` infix, detected via `isSubagentSessionKey()`) continue to preserve their CLI bindings across reset, unchanged from current behaviour. Parent-linked sessions that are NOT spawned subagents (e.g. dashboard children that set `parentSessionKey` without being orchestration-spawned) are treated like any other user-facing session — their bindings get cleared on `/reset`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before (all sessions):
/reset (gateway path) -> openclaw rotates sessionId, archives transcript
                      -> nextEntry still carries claudeCliSessionId=<old>
                      -> next turn spawns: claude --resume <old>
                      -> Anthropic reloads prior transcript from cache
                      -> token meter rebounds to pre-reset level

After:
Non-subagent /reset (e.g. main workspace agent, dashboard child):
                      -> openclaw rotates sessionId, archives transcript
                      -> isSubagentSessionKey(primaryKey)=false -> clearAllCliSessions(nextEntry)
                      -> next turn spawns: claude (no --resume)
                      -> Anthropic mints new sessionId; post-run hook writes it back
                      -> token meter stays at bootstrap baseline

Subagent /reset (canonical `:subagent:` key):
                      -> openclaw rotates sessionId, archives transcript
                      -> isSubagentSessionKey(primaryKey)=true -> guard skips clear
                      -> next turn spawns: claude --resume <old>  (unchanged)
                      -> Tak Hoffman's fa56682b3ced contract preserved
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No (the spawn shape changes for non-subagent resets — `--resume` flag is dropped on the first post-reset turn — but no new endpoint or auth surface; subagent spawn shape is unchanged)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No (the orphaned `<oldSessionId>.jsonl` under `~/.claude/projects/` is unchanged on disk and now simply unreferenced by openclaw's binding for non-subagent sessions; matches pre-existing behaviour for other claude-cli session abandonment paths)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 14.8.x arm64
- Runtime/container: openclaw `2026.4.23`, installed via Homebrew at `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/`
- Model/provider: `claude-opus-4-7` via `claude-cli`
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A (TUI / agent direct)
- Relevant config (redacted): default workspace at `~/.openclaw/workspace`

### Steps

1. In a non-subagent session with non-trivial accumulated context (e.g. ~70% of 1.0m), run `/reset`.
2. Send a follow-up message.
3. Inspect the token meter and the `claudeCliSessionId` field in `sessions.json` for the agent.

### Expected

- Token meter remains at bootstrap baseline across many post-reset turns.
- New `claudeCliSessionId` written by the post-run hook differs from the pre-reset value.
- No `--resume` flag prepended on the first post-reset claude spawn.

### Actual (after this PR)

- All three expectations met for non-subagent sessions. See **Real behavior proof** above for concrete evidence. Subagent sessions retain the pre-existing preservation behaviour.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — pre-fix sessions.json snippet shows preserved bindings; post-fix snippet shows cleared bindings for non-subagent sessions. Happy to paste both (redacted) into a comment if useful.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — see Real behavior proof, "Before evidence" subsection.
- [ ] Screenshot/recording — not included; the meaningful state is in `sessions.json` and the spawned `claude --resume <id>` flag rather than the visual. Can add TUI before/after screenshots on request.
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) — N/A.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `/reset` from a non-subagent session at ~70% context window; one post-reset turn; one follow-up post-reset turn (to confirm the post-run hook writes the new session id back into the entry); `sessions.json` inspection across the reset boundary; `~/.claude/projects/<workspace>/` inspection to confirm new transcript file is created and old one is orphaned (not deleted). Subagent path verified via the focused vitest covering both branches of the guard.
- Edge cases checked: second `/reset` immediately after the first (still clean); resetting a session that had never bound a claude-cli id (no-op, no crash).
- What you did **not** verify: full subagent end-to-end with a live spawned-child claude conversation in a real run (relied on the test); ACP runtime path end-to-end; behaviour under concurrent reset + dispatch; non-claude `cli-session` providers (the fix uses `clearAllCliSessions` which handles all providers uniformly via the existing helper, but I only ran the claude-cli scenario).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

(Pre-checked as a commitment for after CI/bots run. If bot review surfaces issues, I will resolve each one before re-requesting review.)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A
- Note for users with already-stuck non-subagent sessions: this PR fixes the **future** behaviour of `/reset`. Sessions already running with preserved bindings from a pre-fix reset will continue to carry the stale binding until they are reset once more under the fixed code path — at which point the binding is properly cleared. No data migration step required.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a user who was implicitly relying on `/reset` not actually clearing the claude-cli `--resume` binding on non-subagent sessions (e.g. used `/reset` as a no-op refresh of openclaw-side metadata while expecting Anthropic-side continuity) will now see a true clean slate.
  - Mitigation: this is the documented and user-intuitive contract of `/reset`. Anyone relying on the bug was already on accidentally-undefined behaviour. If a soft / fork-from-summary variant is desired as an explicit feature, that's the design space proposed in #81003 (`sessions.hardReset` vs the regular `sessions.reset`) and out of scope for this fix.
- Risk: the subagent guard discriminator is `isSubagentSessionKey(primaryKey)`. If a spawned subagent ever exists under a canonical key that does NOT carry the `:subagent:` infix, this fix would incorrectly clear its bindings.
  - Mitigation: `isSubagentSessionKey` is the same helper already used elsewhere in `session-reset-service.ts` (line 327) as the codebase's canonical subagent test, so any divergence from `:subagent:` keying would be a broader codebase invariant break, not a regression introduced here. If maintainers prefer to also accept `subagentRole` or `spawnedBy` as fallback discriminators, that's a one-line change.
- Risk: `clearAllCliSessions` wipes bindings for **all** CLI providers (claude-cli, codex-cli, etc.), not just claude-cli. For non-subagent `/reset` this is now an upgrade-visible reset contract change for non-claude CLI providers.
  - Mitigation: intentional and consistent with the auto-reply path. Vincent Koc's `7cd015b20398` made the auto-reply `/reset` and `/new` path clear all three binding fields unconditionally (also via the all-providers shape), so this PR's gateway change brings the two paths into alignment for the non-subagent case. If maintainers want to narrow to claude-cli only, swap `clearAllCliSessions(nextEntry)` for `clearCliSession(nextEntry, "claude-cli")` from the same module (also a one-line change).

## Open questions for maintainers (please weigh in)

**Q1 — gateway vs auto-reply asymmetry around subagents:** the guard preserves CLI bindings for spawned subagents on the gateway `/reset` path, matching Tak Hoffman's `fa56682b3ced` contract. The auto-reply path (`src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts` after Vincent Koc's `7cd015b20398`) does **not** carve out a subagent exception — it clears bindings on `/new` and `/reset` unconditionally. Two possible futures:

1. Keep this asymmetry (this PR's current shape): gateway-path resets preserve for spawned subagents; auto-reply resets clear for everything. The two paths intentionally have different contracts because they have different triggers (gateway = explicit API/UI call vs auto-reply = user typing a command in chat).
2. Harmonise: drop the subagent guard in this PR and update Tak's `reset-models.test.ts` test to assert cleared bindings for subagents too. Single contract: every `/reset` clears.

**Q2 — provider-wide clear vs claude-cli-only:** the implementation calls `clearAllCliSessions(nextEntry)`, which wipes bindings for **all** CLI providers (claude-cli, codex-cli, …) on non-subagent reset. This matches the auto-reply path's contract. If you'd rather keep `/reset` scoped to claude-cli only (preserving codex-cli and any other non-claude CLI bindings across reset), the one-line alternative is `clearCliSession(nextEntry, "claude-cli")` from the same `src/agents/cli-session.ts` module.

Happy to take either direction on both — flagging the choices explicitly so they're deliberate maintainer calls rather than accidental gaps.

---

## AI-assist disclosure

This PR was prepared with AI assistance (Claude Opus). The bug was discovered against a real local 4.23 install (`agent:main:axiomyx`, a non-subagent workspace session), root-caused by tracing the cli-runner spawn path through the compiled dist bundle, fixed locally as a hot-patch (preserved at `~/Vork/+OpenClaw/+patches/20260514_v2026.4.23_ResetClearCliSessionBindings/`), and then lifted into this source-level PR. The narrowed-scope decision (preserve for spawned subagents detected via `isSubagentSessionKey()`, clear for everyone else) was made after reading Tak Hoffman's and Vincent Koc's commit history, and tightened from an initial `parentSessionKey || spawnedBy` predicate to `isSubagentSessionKey(primaryKey)` after the first ClawSweeper review correctly pointed out the looser predicate would still exempt user-facing parent-linked sessions like dashboard children. The real behaviour proof above (inspectable artifacts subsection in the Real behavior proof section) is from the local environment, not AI-generated. I have read the change and confirm I understand it. Session logs and the local-hotfix postmortem available on request.

target.title: refactor(config): extract GoogleChat schema into zod-schema.providers-googlechat.ts
target.body:
## Summary

- Extract GoogleChat channel schemas (`GoogleChatDmSchema`, `GoogleChatGroupSchema`, `GoogleChatAccountSchema`, `GoogleChatConfigSchema`) from the monolithic `zod-schema.providers-core.ts` into a dedicated `zod-schema.providers-googlechat.ts` module
- Follows the same pattern as `zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.ts` (already split out)
- Exports `BotLoopProtectionSchema` from `providers-core` since GoogleChat shares it with other channel schemas
- Updates the re-export barrel in `bundled-channel-config-schema.ts`

This is a continuation of the channel schema split pattern established by [#82007](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82007).

## Verification

### Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: code refactor only, no behavior change
Real environment tested: Linux, Node v22
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Schema export verification
node -e "
const tsx = require('tsx/cjs');
const { GoogleChatConfigSchema, GoogleChatDmSchema, GoogleChatGroupSchema, GoogleChatAccountSchema } = require('./src/config/zod-schema.providers-googlechat.ts');
console.log('GoogleChatConfigSchema:', typeof GoogleChatConfigSchema);
console.log('GoogleChatDmSchema:', typeof GoogleChatDmSchema);
console.log('GoogleChatGroupSchema:', typeof GoogleChatGroupSchema);
console.log('GoogleChatAccountSchema:', typeof GoogleChatAccountSchema);
console.log('DmSchema parse:', GoogleChatDmSchema.safeParse({ enabled: true, policy: 'pairing' }).success);
const core = require('./src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts');
console.log('GoogleChat in providers-core:', Object.keys(core).filter(k => k.includes('GoogleChat')).length);
console.log('Other schemas in core:', Object.keys(core).filter(k => k.includes('ConfigSchema')).join(', '));
"

# Contract tests
timeout 120 pnpm test src/plugins/contracts/config-footprint-guardrails.test.ts src/config/config.allowlist-requires-allowfrom.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts
```
Evidence after fix:
```
# Schema export verification
GoogleChatConfigSchema: object
GoogleChatDmSchema: object
GoogleChatGroupSchema: object
GoogleChatAccountSchema: object
DmSchema parse: true
GoogleChat in providers-core: 0 (should be 0)
Other schemas in core: DiscordConfigSchema, IMessageConfigSchema, IrcConfigSchema, MSTeamsConfigSchema, SignalConfigSchema, SlackConfigSchema, TelegramConfigSchema

# Contract tests
 ✓ |contracts-plugin| config-footprint-guardrails.test.ts (7 tests) — all passed
 ✓ |runtime-config| config.allowlist-requires-allowfrom.test.ts (15 tests) — all passed
 ✓ |contracts-plugin| plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts (4 tests) — all passed
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  26 passed (26)
```
Observed result after fix: GoogleChat schemas successfully extracted into their own module with 0 GoogleChat exports remaining in providers-core. All other 7 channel ConfigSchemas remain in providers-core. All 26 contract tests pass.
What was not tested: Full end-to-end gateway startup with GoogleChat channel enabled (requires Google OAuth credentials and webhook setup). CI will validate remaining lanes.

### Additional CI fixes applied
- Fixed `config-footprint-guardrails.test.ts`: regex now matches `providers-googlechat` export block; `toHaveLength(2)` → `3`
- Fixed import boundary in `zod-schema.providers-googlechat.ts`: uses `ChannelBotLoopProtectionSchema` from `zod-schema.channels-config.ts` (neutral module) instead of importing from `zod-schema.providers-core.ts`
- Removed unused `SecretRefSchema` import from `zod-schema.providers-core.ts` (was only referenced by the extracted GoogleChat schemas)

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: fix(cli): avoid false local gateway unreachable on probe timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- separate local gateway reachability from follow-up detail/read probe failures
- add a bounded direct status-RPC fallback or degraded classification for local status/probe paths when the default probe times out or returns unknown capability but the gateway is otherwise RPC-capable
- add regression coverage for the #73535 split: default local probe false-negative while `--require-rpc` or direct `status` RPC succeeds

## Scope
This intentionally excludes unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation from #64400, which is routed to central security triage. The fix should stay in the ordinary status/probe classification and fallback path.

## Credit
Fixes the non-security path tracked in #48360. Uses reproduction evidence from #62762 and #73535, with historical evidence from #51357 and #42019. Thanks @RacecarGuy, @justinschille, @DJBlackhawk, @tianyaqpzm, and @0xrsydn.

## Validation
- `pnpm check:changed`

ProjectClownfish replacement details:
- Cluster: ghcrawl-156625-autonomous-smoke
- Source PRs: none
- Credit: Preserve reporter credit for #48360 by RacecarGuy, #62762 by justinschille, and #73535 by DJBlackhawk in the PR body.; Mention #51357 by tianyaqpzm and #42019 by 0xrsydn as earlier closed reports that supplied useful historical evidence.; Do not claim or implement #64400's unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation; say that security-shaped sub-scope is routed separately.
- Validation: pnpm check:changed

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why
